action.reasoning: Observation: test spearman 0.7322 (best so far) with fine-tuned ESM2-35M and region pooling, but validation spearman 0.8857 indicates overfitting to low-order mutants (two_vs_many split). Training took ~50min for 5 epochs. Analysis: the two_vs_many split requires generalization to higher-order mutants. Subtracting wild-type embeddings (delta approach) forces the model to learn mutation effects rather than absolute fitness, improving generalization. This is a well-known technique in protein fitness prediction. We can implement this by subtracting precomputed wild-type region embedding from each sequence's region embedding before the prediction head. Also, training longer (15 epochs) with early stopping may further improve performance. Decision: modify model and training to incorporate wild-type subtraction, train for 15 epochs (estimated 150min, within 2.8h budget), and submit eval.
